title: Add local time and UTC offset time display below UTC offset selector

requirements.summary: Display current local time below UTC offset selector using the same date-time format shown in the main web layout
acceptanceCriteria: Local time is displayed below the UTC offset selector
Date-time format matches the existing format used elsewhere in the layout

file_operations.description: Add state to track and display current local time in real-time. Add a useEffect hook that updates the local time every second. Position the local time display below the UTC offset selector buttons in the controls overlay div, maintaining the existing layout structure. Use the date-time format already present in the component (e.g., the format used for displaying journey dates or schedule times if available, or create a formatter that matches the application's existing time display pattern).

requirements.summary: Display UTC offset time below local time that dynamically updates when clicking different UTC offset buttons, calculating adjusted time based on selected offset
acceptanceCriteria: UTC offset time appears directly below local time
When a UTC offset button is clicked, the offset time recalculates correctly (e.g., UTC+8 10:19 AM becomes UTC+9 11:19 AM)
Format matches the local time format
Offset time label clearly indicates the selected UTC offset

file_operations.description: Add state to track the currently selected UTC offset (initialize to null or the user's local offset). When a UTC offset button is clicked, update this state with the selected offset value. Add logic to calculate the offset time by adjusting the local time based on the difference between the selected offset and the local timezone offset. Display the calculated offset time directly below the local time, using the same date-time format. Include a label that shows the selected UTC offset (e.g., 'UTC+9 Time: February 23, 2026 at 11:19 AM'). Ensure the offset time updates in real-time along with the local time.

requirements.summary: Position both time displays within the specified XPath element below the UTC offset selector without modifying existing UI layout or 3D globe
acceptanceCriteria: Both time displays are located within the specified XPath element
Times appear directly below the UTC offset selector
No changes are made to the 3D globe rendering or its controls
Existing layout structure and styling remain unchanged

file_operations.description: Locate the controls overlay div that contains the UTC offset selector buttons (this corresponds to the XPath element /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[2]/div[1]/div[3]/div[1]/div[1]). Within this same container div, add a new section immediately after the UTC offset buttons that renders both the local time and UTC offset time displays. Use consistent styling (text size, color, spacing) that matches the existing controls. Ensure no modifications are made to the 3D globe Three.js rendering logic, camera controls, or any globe-related state. Keep all existing layout classes and structure intact, only adding the new time display elements as children of the controls container.
